title: Lead Veterinarian
description:
Mars Veterinary Health (MVH) is a global leading provider of high-quality, advanced veterinary care for companion animals. Our business offers a wide range of high-quality medical services covering preventive and basic health care as well as advanced diagnostics, internal medicine, intensive care, surgery and orthopedics. MVH also provides rehabilitation, physiotherapy, dietary advice and offers selected pet food, pharmaceutical and care products.

Mount Pleasant Veterinary Group (MPVG) is a business unit of MVH Asia, seeks an experienced Lead Veterinarian to lead its veterinary team.

The Lead Veterinarian manages the clinic/s and ensure the highest quality of veterinary care, exceptional client service, associate engagement and maximum productivity of the veterinary medical team. This position advocates for pets and their quality of life; builds a long term bonding relationship with clients; contributes to the development and maintenance of the human-pet bond; educates clients and hospital associates on all aspects of pet health. The Lead Veterinarian partners with the Practice Manager (if applicable) to operate an effective and productive veterinary medical team, ensure a safe and engaging clinic environment, and improve both the medical quality and business performance of the clinic/s.

The ratio of time spent on leadership duties and practicing veterinarian responsibilities will vary depending on the clinic/s situation; the Lead Veterinarian must use good judgment in prioritizing their duties on any given day, ensuring clinic/s productivity. On average, the ratio is expected to be about 20%-30% leadership duties and 70%-80% veterinarian duties.


Leadership Responsibilities (approximately 20-30% of time):

  • Partner with the Practice Manager (if applicable) to manage the clinic/s as role models and champions of the MPVG brand to provide consistent high quality care and exceptional service to the most pets and clients and to maximize efficiency, productivity and profitability. Train the team on MPVG guidelines and practices and supervise associates to ensure compliance.
  • Oversee and train associates on professional, efficient and exceptional client service (lead by example), to include client education about preventive care, pet health needs, clinic/s services, marketing campaigns, and other related information; effectively resolve client issues.
  • Follow all MPVG protocols and practices and be responsible for ensuring that associates achieve practice priorities while building our culture and brand.
  • Identify potential “bottlenecks” and formulate solutions to remove barriers in medical operations that would impede the doctors from providing world class care.
  • Partner with the Director Veterinary Quality, Field Director, and Practice Manager (if applicable) to interview, select, train, develop, coach, mentor and, if necessary, discipline and terminate clinic associates.
  • Direct, lead and mentor Veterinarians to realize their full professional potential and deliver outstanding, high quality medical care, client service and business results.
  • Work with the Director Veterinary Quality to establish development plans (PDPs) for Veterinarians, and focus teams to deliver the practice priorities and exceed goals while building our culture and brand.
  • Effectively create and maintain veterinarians’ schedules and work with Practice Manager to schedule associates to meet client needs according to MPVG guidelines, while proactively planning time off and CE scheduling.
  • Provide effective communication between associates, clients, field leadership and regional support team.
  • Act as communications liaison, including holding routine clinic team meetings, sharing business information and messaging to all clinic associates, ensuring they remain informed, engaged and have opportunities to provide feedback.
  • Provide inspirational leadership to the team by creating a positive professional relationship with the Practice Manager, clients, field leadership and regional support team.
  • Create a collaborative environment and develop an efficient, productive clinic team that provides the highest quality care and service to the most pets and clients.
  • Develop annual planning, budgeting and clinic productivity levels for the clinic in partnership with the Practice Manager (if applicable), Field Director and Director Veterinary Quality.
  • Partner with area Field Director and Director Veterinary Quality to execute market objectives, goals, strategies and measurements (OGSMs).
  • Participate in market level teams, discussions, and initiatives.
  • Partner with the Practice Manager to deliver outstanding financial results for assigned clinic/s.
  • Responsible for the development of technical, medical skills for veterinary assistants/technicians.
  • Responsible for ensuring all associates adhere to MPVG dress and grooming guidelines.
  • Provide leadership to other area clinic/s as needed throughout the market.
  • Ensure compliance with all practice policies and procedures and with all local, state and federal laws.
  • Other assigned duties.

Veterinarian Responsibilities (70-80% of time):

  • Establish trust and gain the confidence and compliance of clients through effectively delivering appropriate preventive care, performing complete diagnostic workups, developing thorough treatment plans, communicating home care instructions, and planning follow-up visits.
  • Assimilate information gathered through effective history taking, physical examination and diagnostic workup to arrive at an appropriate and effective clinical decision.
  • Practice evidence-based medicine and preventive care when data or guidelines exist, using sound judgment when they do not.
  • Practice veterinary medicine in a manner compliant with the Animal & Veterinary Service and Mount Pleasant Veterinary Groups’ (MPVG) Code of Conduct and Ethics, along with appropriate medical record documentation; ensure the clinic team maintains complete medical records that meet practice quality control guidelines.
  • Lead the veterinary clinic team through the daily schedule and caseload, delegating tasks to paraprofessionals and improving clinic efficiency.
  • Provide professional, efficient and exceptional service, including prescribing and administering preventive care for the wellness needs of pets as well as diagnosing and treating diseases and injuries of pets.
  • Advocate for preventive care and adoption of wellness plans.
  • Effectively communicate diagnosis and treatment plan to veterinary medical team and client.
  • Prescribe and administer drugs and vaccines as appropriate.
  • Perform surgery on pets as necessary for the treatment of pets within MPVGs protocols, using standard medical instruments and equipment.
  • Promote the safety of pets, clients and associates by ensuring the clinic team is utilizing safe restraining techniques, following standard practice guidelines, and maintaining clean, sterile and organized treatment areas, exam rooms and laboratory areas.
  • Strive to achieve performance metrics outlined by medical and field leadership.
  • Demonstrate integrity and ethics in all actions and behaviors.
  • Address and resolve client concerns arising from the medical care of a pet.
  • Develop strong lasting relationships with clients.
  • Other assigned duties.

Write to us at MVHAsiaTalent@effem.com if you meet the following criteria:

  • Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM/VMD) or equivalent degree required.
  • Veterinary license in good standing or eligibility for licensure with the applicable veterinary board.
  • Eight years clinical experience required.
  • Minimally three years coaching and developing associate doctors required.
  • Meets all required clinical competencies.
  • Independent, self-motivated, capable to meet tight timeline.
  • Attention to details and accuracy with strong sense of urgency.
  • Ability to handle multi-tasks and respond effectively to ad-hoc requests.
  • Good stakeholder management skills and strong interpersonal and communication skills.

Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further conversation.

postedCompany.description:
Mount Pleasant Veterinary Group has been providing exceptional and compassionate vet care for our animal companions and the community in Singapore for over 30 years. We operate 7 islandwide clinics, including a 24-hour tertiary care centre for patients requiring intensive and critical care.

Our team consists of referral clinicians, senior vets with memberships in small animal internal medicine and surgery, as well as vets with various special interests including avian medicine, dermatology, ophthalmology, surgery and veterinary acupuncture.

We launched our Mount Pleasant Blood Donor Programme in 2016 to save more lives.

On 31 March 2021, Mount Pleasant Veterinary Group joined the Mars Veterinary Health family which includes approximately 70,000+ veterinary professionals globally.


WHY JOIN US?

At Moun Pleasant Veterinary Group (MPVG), we aim to provide progressive and comprehensive care through the practice of evidence-based medicine. We believe that excellence in client and patient care is achieved by investing in continuing education for our associates and in providing the most advance veterinary technology.

We invite you to join our team, to share our love for animals!
postedCompany.name: MOUNT PLEASANT VETERINARY GROUP PTE. LTD.
